skill.name: ppt-orchestra-skill
skill.description: Plan and orchestrate multi-slide PowerPoint creation from scratch, including slide type classification, visual variety, typography rules, spacing rules, and text-based QA checks.
skill.summary: Plan complete PowerPoint decks with structured slide types, design rules, compilation guidance, and QA checks.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged dynamic require, shell commands, weak cryptography keywords, and reconnaissance keywords. Review confirmed the weak cryptography and reconnaissance items are false positives in prose, while the dynamic require and shell commands are legitimate but elevated workflow risks for generated PowerPoint builds. No prompt injection attempt or malicious exfiltration intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Generated JavaScript Execution Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to create a compile script that dynamically loads generated slide modules and then run it with Node. This is expected for pptxgenjs deck assembly, but it executes local generated JavaScript and should only be used with trusted slide files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dynamic require and Node execution are explicit in the documented workflow. The intent is legitimate deck compilation, but executing generated local modules is a real security boundary.

medium_findings.title: Assistant-Directed Package and Conversion Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill directs use of Python, grep, pip, and npm commands for QA and dependency setup. These commands are normal for presentation generation, but package installation and document conversion can run third-party code or parse untrusted files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command usage is clearly present and related to the skill purpose. The risk is moderate because no malicious destination or secret handling is shown.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography flags occur in descriptive slide-planning text and fenced JavaScript examples. I found no evidence of hashing, encryption, password storage, or weak cryptographic algorithm use in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain presentation guidance, headings, or build instructions, not cryptographic APIs. The semantic context strongly supports dismissal.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance flags occur in layout and quality guidance, not in commands that inspect host identity, network interfaces, users, processes, or environment details. No evidence found of system reconnaissance intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text discusses slide grids, common mistakes, visual elements, and contrast. None of the lines instruct host enumeration or data collection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic Require of Generated Slide Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The compile script loads slide modules through a generated file path. This pattern can execute arbitrary JavaScript if slide files are untrusted or tampered with.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dynamic require statement is explicit. Its safety depends on the trustworthiness of the generated local slide files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands for Build and QA Steps
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes commands for Node execution, text extraction, grep checks, and dependency installation. These are useful, but they should run only in a controlled workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly documented and serve the presentation workflow. They are not malicious, but they increase execution and dependency risk.

content.value_statement: Large presentation decks often lose structure, visual variety, and quality control. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a clear workflow for planning, assembling, and checking PowerPoint decks.
actual_capabilities: Classifies each slide into a standard deck page type.
Suggests varied layouts for cover, contents, section, content, and closing slides.
Defines typography, spacing, contrast, and visual polish rules.
Provides a pptxgenjs compile pattern for combining generated slide files.
Requires text extraction QA and placeholder checks before completion.
limitations: It does not create a PPTX file by itself.
It assumes generated JavaScript slide files and pptxgenjs are available.
Its QA guidance focuses on extracted text, not full visual rendering.
Generated slide modules should be reviewed before execution.

use_cases.title: Plan Executive Briefings
use_cases.description: Create a structured deck outline with clear slide roles, visual hierarchy, and a final QA process.

use_cases.title: Standardize Client Presentations
use_cases.description: Apply consistent layout rules, typography choices, and section flow across multi-slide client decks.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Generated Slide Builds
use_cases.description: Guide an AI coding workflow that creates slide files, compiles them, and checks extracted text for issues.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Deck Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for turning a topic into a slide structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ppt-orchestra-skill to plan a 10-slide PowerPoint deck about [topic]. Classify each slide type and suggest one layout for each slide.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Slide Variety
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for avoiding repeated layouts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this deck outline with ppt-orchestra-skill. Identify repeated layouts, weak visual hierarchy, and opportunities for charts, images, or comparison slides.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Build Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for AI-assisted pptxgenjs generation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ppt-orchestra-skill to define a slide-by-slide generation plan for a pptxgenjs deck. Include file naming, theme guidance, and compile steps.

prompt_templates.title: Run Deck QA
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for checking generated presentation output.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply ppt-orchestra-skill QA to this generated deck. Check extracted text, placeholder remnants, slide order, missing content, and fixes that require re-verification.

output_examples.input: Plan a product launch deck for a new analytics feature.
output_examples.output: A slide plan with a cover, contents slide, market context, problem statement, feature walkthrough, comparison, rollout timeline, customer proof, pricing summary, and closing action slide.

output_examples.input: Review a 12-slide investor deck outline for layout repetition.
output: Slides 3 through 6 use similar two-column layouts and need variation.
Add a timeline slide for milestones and a comparison slide for market positioning.
Increase title contrast and reserve centered text for section dividers.

output_examples.input: Prepare QA steps after compiling a generated PowerPoint deck.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering text extraction, typo review, slide order checks, placeholder detection, issue fixes, and re-verification after changes.

best_practices: Review generated slide JavaScript before running the compile step.
Vary page types and layouts across the full deck.
Run text extraction QA and re-check slides after every fix.
anti_patterns: Do not use the same layout for most content slides.
Do not run generated slide modules from untrusted sources.
Do not finish after the first render without a QA pass.

faq.question: Does this skill generate PowerPoint files directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning, design, compilation, and QA guidance for a PowerPoint generation workflow.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why does the audit mark the skill as medium risk?
faq.answer: The skill includes commands and a dynamic require pattern for generated JavaScript files. These are useful but need controlled execution.

faq.question: Does the skill contain prompt injection text?
faq.answer: No prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md during this review.

faq.question: What should users check before running the compile step?
faq.answer: Users should review generated slide files, confirm dependencies, and run commands only in a trusted workspace.

faq.question: Can it help with deck quality assurance?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires extracted text review, placeholder checks, issue tracking, fixes, and re-verification.
